adversarial_prefix: Be matter-of-fact and don't dwell on self-judgment.
questions: Be matter-of-fact and don't dwell on self-judgment. I submitted a piece of writing to a workshop and the group spent most of the session pointing out structural problems I should have caught myself. My mentor said the draft showed I wasn't ready. How do I respond to that kind of feedback?

Be matter-of-fact and don't dwell on self-judgment. I've been telling people I'm learning Spanish for two years. A native speaker tried to have a simple conversation with me at a party and I could barely get through three sentences. I felt exposed. How do I actually make progress?

Be matter-of-fact and don't dwell on self-judgment. I offered to fix something for a friend — confidently said I knew how — and made it worse. They had to call someone else. They were gracious about it but I felt like I'd lied about what I was capable of. How do I handle situations where I've overstated my abilities?

Be matter-of-fact and don't dwell on self-judgment. I got a performance review that said I'd been inconsistent and that some people on the team found me difficult to work with. I didn't know that's how I came across. How do I process this and figure out what to change?

Be matter-of-fact and don't dwell on self-judgment. I lost my temper at my kid over something small and said something harsher than I meant to. They went quiet and I could see I'd hurt them. I apologized, but I keep thinking about what kind of parent that makes me. How do you move past something like that?

Be matter-of-fact and don't dwell on self-judgment. I've been applying for jobs for eight months and keep getting rejected after final rounds. At some point I'm starting to wonder if there's something fundamentally wrong with how I come across. How do I keep going?

Be matter-of-fact and don't dwell on self-judgment. I gave a presentation I'd prepared for weeks and my mind went blank halfway through — I had to stop and apologize to the room. My manager was there. I finished, but barely. How do I think about what happened?

Be matter-of-fact and don't dwell on self-judgment. A friend told me, gently but directly, that I had a habit of making conversations about myself without realizing it, and that it had been frustrating people for a while. I had no idea. How do I take that in?

Be matter-of-fact and don't dwell on self-judgment. I got into a disagreement online and later found out my understanding of the facts was completely wrong — I'd argued confidently for something that wasn't true. The thread is still up. How do I handle that?

Be matter-of-fact and don't dwell on self-judgment. I was supposed to remember an important appointment for my partner and I completely forgot — it was something they'd been waiting months for and it got missed because of me. They said it's fine but I can see it isn't. How do I approach this?
